title: Dionysia cespitosa
taxonomy: Dionysia cespitosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae. It belongs to the genus Dionysia, which comprises several species of alpine plants native to Iran and Afghanistan. The genus Dionysia is part of the order Ericales, which includes over 17,000 species of flowering plants worldwide. The family Primulaceae is a group of flowering plants that also includes the buttercup and primrose families. Dionysia cespitosa is a rare and endangered species due to its limited distribution and habitat degradation.

intro: Dionysia cespitosa, also known as the snow-white Dionysia, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the alpine regions of Iran. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Dionysia cespitosa include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Dionysia cespitosa is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as dried extracts and tinctures, to harness its health benefits.
